risks: Commodity price volatility due to weather, climate change, product scarcity, limited sources of supply, commodity market fluctuations, currency fluctuations, trade tariffs, pandemics, war, and changes in governmental agricultural and energy policies

Disruption of supply chain due to weather, climate change, natural disaster, fire, terrorism, cyber-attack, pandemics, war, governmental restrictions or mandates, labor shortages, strikes, import/export restrictions
Many product lines manufactured at a single location or sourced from a single supplier
Failure of third-party suppliers, contract manufacturers, commercial transport, distributors, contractors, and external business partners to meet obligations
Short term or sustained increases in consumer demand may exceed production capacity or strain supply chain

regulatory_risks: Compliance with laws and regulations administered by USDA, FDA, OSHA, EPA, and other federal, state, local, and foreign governmental agencies relating to production, packaging, labelling, storage, distribution, quality, and safety of food products
New laws or regulations restricting marketing or sale of products due to ingredients or substances in products or product packaging

Requirements to highlight perceived concerns about a product or product packaging, warn consumers to avoid consumption of certain ingredients or substances, restrict audience to whom products are marketed or sold, limit locations where products may be available, or discontinue use of certain ingredients or packaging

Environmental laws and regulations (Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Resource Conservation and Recovery Act, Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act, Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act)
Greenhouse gas emission regulations (e.g., carbon taxes)
Disclosure obligations related to climate change or other sustainability issues
False or deceptive advertising claims under federal, state, and foreign laws and regulations
Trade tariffs on products and ingredients (including recent tariffs imposed or threatened to be imposed by the United States on China, Canada, Mexico, and other countries and any retaliatory actions)
Nationalization or government control of operations
Exchange controls and currency exchange rates
Restriction on the transfer of funds to and from foreign countries

sanction_risks: Sanctions imposed on Russia for its invasion of Ukraine
Tariffs imposed or threatened to be imposed by the United States on China, Canada, Mexico, and other countries and any retaliatory actions taken by such countries
